Lagaan's anniversary celebrates not just a blockbuster but a cultural moment that took Indian cinema to global audiences in 2001.
stories.lens: Lagaan's 25th anniversary at an international festival underlines how a single film can sustain India's global cultural presence for a quarter-century — and Aamir Khan's attendance keeps that legacy firmly in the spotlight.
stories.article:
Aamir Khan will travel to Melbourne to attend the Indian Film Festival of Melbourne's curtain raiser event, marking 25 years of Lagaan — the film that brought Indian cinema one of its most celebrated Oscar nominations and captured audiences across the world.

Released in 2001, Lagaan was nominated for the Academy Award for Best Foreign Language Film and became a phenomenon far beyond Bollywood's usual reach. The film's sweeping narrative of a village challenging British colonial rule through a cricket match resonated with audiences globally, cementing Aamir Khan's reputation as one of Hindi cinema's most ambitious and meticulous storytellers.

The Indian Film Festival of Melbourne, one of the most prominent platforms for Indian cinema in the Southern Hemisphere, has chosen the Lagaan anniversary as its marquee occasion for the curtain raiser, reflecting the film's enduring significance. Aamir Khan's in-person attendance signals the importance both the festival and the star attach to this milestone.

The 25th anniversary comes at a moment when nostalgia for early-2000s Bollywood classics is running high among Indian audiences at home and in the diaspora. For millions who grew up watching Lagaan, the occasion is as much a celebration of an era as of a film.
key_facts: Aamir Khan to attend IFFM curtain raiser in Melbourne
Lagaan completes 25 years since its 2001 release
Lagaan was nominated for the Oscar for Best Foreign Language Film

stories.headline: Jaya Janaki Nayaka Becomes Highest-Watched Indian Film on YouTube; Rakul Preet Singh Celebrates
stories.what: Telugu film Jaya Janaki Nayaka has achieved the landmark of becoming the highest-watched Indian film on YouTube, a milestone that actress Rakul Preet Singh — one of its stars — took to social media to celebrate. The 2017 romantic action film, featuring Bellamkonda Sai Sreenivas and Rakul Preet Singh, has now surpassed all rivals on the platform in terms of viewership. The achievement underlines the extraordinary reach of Telugu cinema on digital platforms.
stories.lens: Jaya Janaki Nayaka's YouTube record is a vivid reminder of how Telugu cinema has built a massive global digital audience — and how platforms like YouTube are reshaping which films endure in popular memory.
stories.article:
Jaya Janaki Nayaka has been crowned the highest-watched Indian film on YouTube, a record milestone that actress Rakul Preet Singh celebrated publicly, marking a remarkable achievement for the Telugu film industry's digital footprint.

The 2017 romantic action entertainer, which starred Bellamkonda Sai Sreenivas and Rakul Preet Singh, has accumulated viewership on YouTube that surpasses every other Indian film on the platform — a testament to the enduring popularity of Telugu mass entertainers with diaspora and domestic audiences alike. Rakul shared her excitement on social media, calling attention to what the record represents for the film and its team.

The milestone reflects a broader trend: Telugu and other South Indian films have consistently outperformed Bollywood productions on streaming and digital platforms, drawing on enormous fan bases in Andhra Pradesh, Telangana, and among the global Telugu-speaking community. YouTube's free-access model has made it a particularly powerful vehicle for this reach.

For Rakul Preet Singh, who has since built a prominent cross-industry career in both Telugu and Hindi films, the record is a nostalgic marker of one of her most beloved earlier performances. The news has sparked a wave of celebration among the film's fans online.
key_facts: Jaya Janaki Nayaka named highest-watched Indian film on YouTube
Film starred Bellamkonda Sai Sreenivas and Rakul Preet Singh (2017)
Rakul Preet Singh celebrated the milestone on social media

stories.headline: Neha Dhupia Loses Temper at Paparazzi Over 'Disrespectful' Back Shots
stories.what: Actress Neha Dhupia confronted paparazzi photographers, losing her temper over what she described as 'disrespectful' back shots — photographs taken from behind without her consent. The incident has reignited debate about the boundaries of celebrity photography and the ethics of paparazzi culture in Bollywood. Dhupia's public reaction is being seen as a firm pushback against invasive photographic practices that many actors have quietly tolerated for years.
stories.lens: Neha Dhupia's confrontation raises important questions about consent and dignity in celebrity coverage — a conversation that has real implications for how the media and public engage with public figures.
stories.article:
Neha Dhupia publicly lost her temper at paparazzi photographers after they took what she called 'disrespectful' back shots — photographs taken from behind, without her engagement or consent. The incident, which quickly circulated on social media, has sparked a fresh debate about the ethics and limits of celebrity photography in India.

Dhupia made her displeasure clear in no uncertain terms, confronting the photographers on the spot. Her objection centred on the nature of the images — taken from behind and, in her view, demeaning — rather than simply the act of being photographed. The episode highlights a tension that simmers constantly between Bollywood celebrities and the paparazzi ecosystem that surrounds them.

While stars routinely engage with photographers at events and airports, many have long expressed discomfort with unsolicited or invasive shots. Dhupia's willingness to call it out publicly, using the word 'disrespectful', brings an ethical dimension to what is often dismissed as a routine celebrity-media friction.

The moment is part of a broader reckoning in the industry about boundaries, consent and how public figures — particularly women — are photographed and represented. Dhupia's reaction has drawn both support and debate online, with many women in and outside the industry voicing solidarity.
key_facts: Neha Dhupia confronted paparazzi over 'disrespectful' back shots
She objected to being photographed from behind without consent
Incident reignites debate over ethics of celebrity photography in India

stories.headline: Abhishek Bachchan Opens Up About Battling Insecurity in Bollywood: 'Kaam Milega Ya Nahi'
stories.what: Abhishek Bachchan has candidly recalled the deep professional insecurity he faced during his Bollywood career, articulating the constant anxiety in his own words: 'Kaam milega ya nahi' — will I get work or not. The actor, who has navigated the unique pressures of being the son of megastar Amitabh Bachchan, spoke about the psychological weight of uncertainty in an industry where careers are notoriously volatile. His remarks offer a rare, unguarded look at the human cost of surviving in Hindi cinema.
stories.lens: Abhishek Bachchan's candid admission resonates with millions of Indians who face job insecurity — and reminds audiences that even celebrity and a famous surname cannot shield anyone from professional self-doubt.
stories.article:
Abhishek Bachchan has spoken with striking candour about the professional insecurity that dogged his years in Bollywood, putting his anxiety into plain words: 'Kaam milega ya nahi' — will I get work or not. The admission, shared in a recent interview, strips away the glamour often associated with star-kid careers and reveals the very human fear of irrelevance that haunts even established actors.

For Abhishek, the pressure has always been compounded by the towering legacy of his father, Amitabh Bachchan — one of Hindi cinema's greatest icons. Being a Bachchan brought visibility but also relentless scrutiny, with every film and performance measured against an almost impossible standard. Navigating that dynamic while building an independent identity required confronting self-doubt head-on.

The actor has had a career of considerable range, from commercial entertainers to critically appreciated roles, but has also weathered lean patches and public criticism that would test anyone's resolve. His willingness to name the insecurity directly — rather than project an image of unshakeable confidence — has drawn empathy from fans and industry observers.

The conversation reflects a broader shift in how Bollywood celebrities are discussing mental health and professional vulnerability. Abhishek Bachchan's openness adds a valuable, grounded voice to that evolving dialogue.
key_facts: Abhishek Bachchan recalled career insecurity in Bollywood
His words: 'Kaam milega ya nahi' (will I get work or not)
Spoke about unique pressures of being Amitabh Bachchan's son
